themes.mechanism: Set `data-theme` on <html>. 'system' (default) follows the OS and is resolved to light/dark in JS (src/lib/theme.ts); a flash-free boot script in index.html paints it before first render. The Tailwind semantic colors read CSS variables, so both themes flow from one set of tokens.

light.displayName: The Daylit Station (warm-paper chrome framing a white note page)

rules: Two ambers: `--accent` is the bright signal for FILLS/graphics (Play, spines, dots, progress); `--accent-ink` is the AA-safe amber for TEXT/LED/links/focus. Identical in dark, divergent in light (bright amber fails AA as text on a pale surface).

The note page (`--note` / `bg-note`) is the warm base tone in light (NOT stark white — that read too clinical against the warm theme); it equals ink in dark. It is the deepest warm surface, with chrome panels lifting lighter around it.
`--on-bright` is the text that sits ON an amber/hue fill (timecode labels, the Play button): dark in both themes.
`--row-sel` is the selected/active note row: = raised in dark, a warm highlight on light (raised would vanish into the white page).
Note/tag/element hues stay raw as FILLS; used as TEXT or a 1px border on the white page they are mixed toward ink for AA (src/lib/noteColors.ts `hueText`).
Every light pair is WCAG AA verified: body/muted/placeholder/amber-text >= 4.5:1, non-text graphics >= 3:1.

display.purpose: The editable track title; the largest prose on screen. A tool, so it never shouts (<=1.125rem).

body.purpose: Note content and general UI copy; system humanist sans, ~65-75ch measure.

label.purpose: Panel title bars, badges, micro-controls. Monospace, uppercase, +0.2em.

mono.purpose: The transport/header clock and timecode tags. Amber, tabular, glowing.

shadows.name: inset-bevel
shadows.purpose: Recessed screens only: LED readouts, the meter well, inputs, the editor canvas.

shadows.name: raised-bevel
shadows.purpose: The primary Play button only. A hint of a physical key, never a gel button.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default hover/active feedback on controls. Quiet and immediate.

motion.name: seg-flicker
motion.purpose: Segmented LED meter shimmer; staggered per segment to read like a VU meter.

components.name: Play Button
components.description: The one filled control in the transport; toggles playback.

components.name: Capture Button
components.description: Primary creative action: add a note at the current playhead. Amber outline, not a second solid-amber control.

components.name: LED Readout
components.description: Recessed amber clock; the transport and header timecode.

components.name: Panel Title Bar
components.description: The load-bearing instrument-panel header worn by every panel.

components.name: Note Row
components.description: Signature component: a flush cue-list row with a colored identity spine and a mono timecode tag. Not a card.
components.html:
● live
Famous opening motif: short-short-short-long.

components.name: Track Row
components.description: Flush sidebar track entry; active state carries a 2px amber left-bar.
components.html: ▶Beethoven – Symphony No. 5

narrative.overview: Sound Annotator is built like an audio analysis bench, not a web app. The surface is warm-dark and quiet so the eye rests on the recording and the notes, not the chrome. Everything reads like instrument output: timecodes and counts are monospace, the transport carries an LED clock and a level meter, panels are flush metal with hard edges. It is dense by intent, optimized for a teacher who returns daily and knows it cold. Color is data, not decoration: one signal color, amber, means 'now', while the note palette encodes identity and is always paired with a timecode label so meaning survives a projector or a colorblind viewer.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-dark, four-step tonal surface ramp; depth from tone, not shadow.
One amber signal color meaning 'now'; everything else neutral or data-hue.
Monospace tabular numerics for every timecode, count, and micro-label.
Squared corners (<=2px), flush panels, hairline dividers; zero card-gaps.
Secondary controls hidden at rest, revealed on hover or focus.

rules.name: The Amber-Is-Now Rule
rules.body: Amber means the present moment or the primary action, nothing else. Forbidden as a decorative fill. If more than ~10% of a screen is amber, something non-temporal has stolen the signal color.

rules.name: The Color-Is-Data Rule
rules.body: Hue only encodes identity (which note), never emotion or hierarchy. Any color-coded element must also carry a text label (timecode), so it reads on a dim projector and for colorblind users.

rules.name: The Monospace-Numerics Rule
rules.body: Every timecode, duration, and count is monospace and tabular-figure; numbers never reflow. Prose is never monospace; numerics are never proportional.

rules.name: The No-Shouting Rule
rules.body: Product UI: display type stays <=1.25rem. No hero type. Hierarchy comes from weight, case, and the mono/sans split, not size.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Reach for the surface ramp before any shadow. To separate two surfaces, change tone or draw a hairline; do not float one on a shadow.

rules.name: The Bevel-Restraint Rule
rules.body: Bevels are 1px lighting hints on interactive controls only. Textures, surface gradients, gloss, and faux-material finishes are forbidden. If a bevel looks like brushed metal, it has failed.

dos: Do build depth from the tonal ramp (ink -> panel -> raised -> inset) and hairline (#38342b) dividers. Flush panels, squared corners (<=2px).
Do keep numerics monospace and tabular: timecodes, counts, durations.
Do reserve Signal Amber (#f5a623) for 'now' and the primary action; keep it under ~10% of any screen.
Do pair every color-coded note with its timecode label, so identity never depends on hue alone (projector + colorblind safe).
Do hide secondary controls until hover/focus; let the resting UI stay calm.
donts: Don't ship the generic AI / shadcn dashboard look: rounded cards, soft drop shadows, indigo accent, evenly padded 'friendly SaaS' whitespace.
Don't drift into heavy enterprise SaaS clutter: gray-on-gray toolbars, competing controls, data-grid busyness.
Don't use retro skeuomorphism: fake wood or metal textures, glossy bevels, brushed-aluminum.
Don't let color carry meaning alone, and don't add a second face to the type system. Two voices (sans + mono) only.
Don't introduce a second solid-amber control next to Play, or round a corner past 2px.
